content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣Ba
2	
3	## Shape
4	
5	This heat creates the **worksite repo** and the **atomic Rust manipulators** that own
6	its read/write/commit, and **rebalances the fundus/curia/BURP/BURN files** into the
7	worksite model (name provisional): an operator-scoped, repo-shaped home that any number
8	of discrete projects share. It carries an unresolved design core — chiefly the
9	worksite's git-database representation — and is stood up now to **hold** the decisions
10	and open questions below until the operator can focus on resolving them. Today's act is
11	capture, not construction; the build waits until the design core settles.
12	
13	Spine is the worksite model. BURN/BURP (fleet topology) and curia/fundus (operation
14	roles) are reorganized into it, not merely referenced.
15	
16	## Cinched
17	
18	- **Decouple JJ state from work repos** into a shared singleton worksite. The married
19	  model contaminates the work repo's git preconditions — the pensum-seed eviction is
20	  the prior symptom (JJ bookkeeping commits advanced HEAD past origin and tripped
21	  foray's curia-readiness guard). Decoupling dissolves that contamination class, not
22	  just one instance. This is the case for decoupling, weighted above merge relief.
23	- **Git stays source-of-truth and journal — not a transactional store.** The covenant
24	  survives: additive-only history, parseable commit-as-intent, jjx_log reconstruction,
25	  free immutability. A transactional store would force re-establishing all of them.
26	- **Central/shared across stations, synced by a JJ-owned pull-rebase-push loop.** The
27	  human never hand-manages the worksite's git. Git push is an atomic compare-and-swap
28	  on the remote; that CAS is the cross-station lock.
29	- **Writers must be isolatable; single-blob storage is disqualifying.** Single-blob is
30	  the shared root of merge pressure, global-lock contention, and round-trip-validation
31	  cost — and under shared-sync it fails outright (every write contends on one file;
32	  every rebase is a 3-way merge of canonical JSON). Two stations on different heats must
33	  push without conflict. Per-heat sharding is the natural mechanism; the exact
34	  representation is the git-database fork (Held) — principle is cinched, mechanism open.
35	- **Exclusivity is soft.** Reservation tokens grant node ownership; operations
36	  expire/reissue them; an operator can administratively reclaim a forgotten
37	  reservation. No distributed-consensus machinery — fits sole-operator.
38	- **Asymmetric sync.** The worksite (authoritative) is tight: gallops lifecycle ops
39	  bracket as pull-rebase -> lock -> mutate -> push -> unlock. Repos-under-test are
40	  SHA-referenced only and sync lazily (eventually-consistent).
41	- **Generalize, don't invent, for cross-repo references.** The basis field (per-tack
42	  HEAD SHA, with the unknown-sentinel for "can't determine") is already a (repo, SHA)
43	  reference — lift it to (work-repo, SHA). The legatio/BURN registry already names
44	  external targets — the work-repo registry is its sibling.
45	- **The legatio (alias, reldir) pair is the factoring:** alias -> fleet topology
46	  (leaves the repo, operator-scoped); reldir -> project location (stays with project).
47	- **BURN/BURP is BUK substrate JJ defers to**, not JJ-owned. The rebalance relocates
48	  these files into the worksite home (co-residence), but JJ resolves by alias and owns
49	  nothing of their semantics — co-residence, not absorption.
50	- **Worksite git-engine = vvx (Rust); BUK owns fleet-artifact semantics, not git
51	  mutation.** Verified grain: BUK does no git mutation today (only git status); all
52	  mutation plus the lock (refs/vvg/locks/vvx) lives in the Rust vvc crate; vvx already
53	  reads BUK's burn.env artifacts directly. One engine, one lock — two git drivers on a
54	  co-tenant store would race on a single station the way two stations would.
55	- **Read/write asymmetry — many readers, one writer.** BUK may peek the worksite, but
56	  only its own domain (fleet topology, reservation tokens) directly. Gallops awareness
57	  for BUK routes through a vvx read path — never raw JSON (Exclusive JSON Ownership).
58	  Reads take no lock and tolerate slight staleness.
59	- **Worksite + JJ core are project-agnostic, zero RBK coupling.** JJ is more general
60	  than RBK (used on any tracked project); the right relayering is extraction/delivery
61	  boundaries, NOT a runtime RBK->JJ dependency. RBK and JJ stay dependency-independent
62	  siblings on BUK. The cross-project worksite forces this. JJ's core is even
63	  BUK-independent; only foray reaches BUK artifacts.
64	
65	## Held (refine before cutting paces)
66	
67	- **Worksite git-database representation — the design core that gates the build.** A
68	  peer to the gallops JSON (further JSON stores committed alongside), or something more
69	  primal (git objects/refs as the database directly). Git-stays-truth holds either way;
70	  this decision sets the data shape and subsumes the sharding mechanism above.
71	- **Scope boundary of Ba vs follow-on heats.** Which of {gallops sharding rollout,
72	  station-partitioned minting, the full cross-station pull-rebase-push sync loop} land
73	  in this heat versus later. Settled once the git-database fork resolves.
74	- **Advisory vs. authoritative tracked SHA.** Lean: advisory by default (degrades
75	  gracefully under rebase-away, as basis does today), with foray's curia-readiness
76	  guard the only authoritative point. Governs the entire reconciliation burden.
77	- **Station-partitioned firemark/coronet minting.** Root state (next_heat_seed,
78	  heat_order) is the irreducible shared point even with per-heat shards. Needs a
79	  partition scheme so heat creation requires no cross-station coordination; the
80	  pensum-seed "cross-officium collision acceptable" stance is the template.
81	- **Conflict policy** when two stations touch the same shard: CAS rejects, JJ retries a
82	  small number of times, then recommends an administrative action (still within the
83	  system). Open: what happens to a genuine content conflict on one heat-file.
84	- **curia/fundus re-focus.** The curia is a hub with two transports: foray/SSH to fundi
85	  (work execution), git to the worksite (state). Open: is the worksite a new role
86	  alongside curia/fundus, or curia-owned infrastructure? Reclaiming/reusing
87	  curia/fundus is in scope.
88	- **Project dimension in gallops.** With many projects sharing one worksite: does a
89	  heat carry an explicit project, or is project implicit via the work-repo its paces'
90	  SHAs reference?
91	- **Naming.** "worksite" and any new role term are provisional. Minting (namespace
92	  enumeration, terminal exclusivity, equestrian/nautical fit — note fundus already =
93	  Latin "estate") happens once the model sets, not now.
94	- **Lock-as-protocol escape hatch.** If a real "fleet op must run where vvx is absent"
95	  context appears, define the worksite lock as a language-agnostic protocol (lock-ref +
96	  CAS-on-push discipline) so a thin bash driver could participate without code-sharing
97	  or inverting the layer stack. Hold; do not build speculatively.
98	- **vvx/JJ extraction from the RBK-centric monorepo** — delivery/workspace decoupling.
99	  On the operator's mind; deferred.
100	
101	## Done when
102	
103	The worksite repo exists and its atomic Rust manipulators (validated read/write/commit
104	plus lock) are built and tested; the fundus/curia/BURP/BURN files are rebalanced into
105	the worksite model; the git-database representation and the other Held forks are
106	resolved-and-recorded; and the Ba-vs-follow-on scope boundary is decided. No
107	construction begins until the design core (the git-database approach) is settled.
108	
109	## Character
110	
111	Design requiring judgment, then a careful build — architectural model-setting plus the
112	worksite repo and its atomic manipulators. Standing now as a holding pen for settled
113	decisions and open forks; construction waits until the operator can focus and resolve
114	the git-database core. Resist building before the model sets.
115

# jjezs_paddock ₣Ba

## Shape

This heat creates the **worksite repo** and the **atomic Rust manipulators** that own
its read/write/commit, and **rebalances the fundus/curia/BURP/BURN files** into the
worksite model (name provisional): an operator-scoped, repo-shaped home that any number
of discrete projects share. It carries an unresolved design core — chiefly the
worksite's git-database representation — and is stood up now to **hold** the decisions
and open questions below until the operator can focus on resolving them. Today's act is
capture, not construction; the build waits until the design core settles.

Spine is the worksite model. BURN/BURP (fleet topology) and curia/fundus (operation
roles) are reorganized into it, not merely referenced.

## Cinched

- **Decouple JJ state from work repos** into a shared singleton worksite. The married
  model contaminates the work repo's git preconditions — the pensum-seed eviction is
  the prior symptom (JJ bookkeeping commits advanced HEAD past origin and tripped
  foray's curia-readiness guard). Decoupling dissolves that contamination class, not
  just one instance. This is the case for decoupling, weighted above merge relief.
- **Git stays source-of-truth and journal — not a transactional store.** The covenant
  survives: additive-only history, parseable commit-as-intent, jjx_log reconstruction,
  free immutability. A transactional store would force re-establishing all of them.
- **Central/shared across stations, synced by a JJ-owned pull-rebase-push loop.** The
  human never hand-manages the worksite's git. Git push is an atomic compare-and-swap
  on the remote; that CAS is the cross-station lock.
- **Writers must be isolatable; single-blob storage is disqualifying.** Single-blob is
  the shared root of merge pressure, global-lock contention, and round-trip-validation
  cost — and under shared-sync it fails outright (every write contends on one file;
  every rebase is a 3-way merge of canonical JSON). Two stations on different heats must
  push without conflict. Per-heat sharding is the natural mechanism; the exact
  representation is the git-database fork (Held) — principle is cinched, mechanism open.
- **Exclusivity is soft.** Reservation tokens grant node ownership; operations
  expire/reissue them; an operator can administratively reclaim a forgotten
  reservation. No distributed-consensus machinery — fits sole-operator.
- **Asymmetric sync.** The worksite (authoritative) is tight: gallops lifecycle ops
  bracket as pull-rebase -> lock -> mutate -> push -> unlock. Repos-under-test are
  SHA-referenced only and sync lazily (eventually-consistent).
- **Generalize, don't invent, for cross-repo references.** The basis field (per-tack
  HEAD SHA, with the unknown-sentinel for "can't determine") is already a (repo, SHA)
  reference — lift it to (work-repo, SHA). The legatio/BURN registry already names
  external targets — the work-repo registry is its sibling.
- **The legatio (alias, reldir) pair is the factoring:** alias -> fleet topology
  (leaves the repo, operator-scoped); reldir -> project location (stays with project).
- **BURN/BURP is BUK substrate JJ defers to**, not JJ-owned. The rebalance relocates
  these files into the worksite home (co-residence), but JJ resolves by alias and owns
  nothing of their semantics — co-residence, not absorption.
- **Worksite git-engine = vvx (Rust); BUK owns fleet-artifact semantics, not git
  mutation.** Verified grain: BUK does no git mutation today (only git status); all
  mutation plus the lock (refs/vvg/locks/vvx) lives in the Rust vvc crate; vvx already
  reads BUK's burn.env artifacts directly. One engine, one lock — two git drivers on a
  co-tenant store would race on a single station the way two stations would.
- **Read/write asymmetry — many readers, one writer.** BUK may peek the worksite, but
  only its own domain (fleet topology, reservation tokens) directly. Gallops awareness
  for BUK routes through a vvx read path — never raw JSON (Exclusive JSON Ownership).
  Reads take no lock and tolerate slight staleness.
- **Worksite + JJ core are project-agnostic, zero RBK coupling.** JJ is more general
  than RBK (used on any tracked project); the right relayering is extraction/delivery
  boundaries, NOT a runtime RBK->JJ dependency. RBK and JJ stay dependency-independent
  siblings on BUK. The cross-project worksite forces this. JJ's core is even
  BUK-independent; only foray reaches BUK artifacts.

## Held (refine before cutting paces)

- **Worksite git-database representation — the design core that gates the build.** A
  peer to the gallops JSON (further JSON stores committed alongside), or something more
  primal (git objects/refs as the database directly). Git-stays-truth holds either way;
  this decision sets the data shape and subsumes the sharding mechanism above.
- **Scope boundary of Ba vs follow-on heats.** Which of {gallops sharding rollout,
  station-partitioned minting, the full cross-station pull-rebase-push sync loop} land
  in this heat versus later. Settled once the git-database fork resolves.
- **Advisory vs. authoritative tracked SHA.** Lean: advisory by default (degrades
  gracefully under rebase-away, as basis does today), with foray's curia-readiness
  guard the only authoritative point. Governs the entire reconciliation burden.
- **Station-partitioned firemark/coronet minting.** Root state (next_heat_seed,
  heat_order) is the irreducible shared point even with per-heat shards. Needs a
  partition scheme so heat creation requires no cross-station coordination; the
  pensum-seed "cross-officium collision acceptable" stance is the template.
- **Conflict policy** when two stations touch the same shard: CAS rejects, JJ retries a
  small number of times, then recommends an administrative action (still within the
  system). Open: what happens to a genuine content conflict on one heat-file.
- **curia/fundus re-focus.** The curia is a hub with two transports: foray/SSH to fundi
  (work execution), git to the worksite (state). Open: is the worksite a new role
  alongside curia/fundus, or curia-owned infrastructure? Reclaiming/reusing
  curia/fundus is in scope.
- **Project dimension in gallops.** With many projects sharing one worksite: does a
  heat carry an explicit project, or is project implicit via the work-repo its paces'
  SHAs reference?
- **Naming.** "worksite" and any new role term are provisional. Minting (namespace
  enumeration, terminal exclusivity, equestrian/nautical fit — note fundus already =
  Latin "estate") happens once the model sets, not now.
- **Lock-as-protocol escape hatch.** If a real "fleet op must run where vvx is absent"
  context appears, define the worksite lock as a language-agnostic protocol (lock-ref +
  CAS-on-push discipline) so a thin bash driver could participate without code-sharing
  or inverting the layer stack. Hold; do not build speculatively.
- **vvx/JJ extraction from the RBK-centric monorepo** — delivery/workspace decoupling.
  On the operator's mind; deferred.

## Done when

The worksite repo exists and its atomic Rust manipulators (validated read/write/commit
plus lock) are built and tested; the fundus/curia/BURP/BURN files are rebalanced into
the worksite model; the git-database representation and the other Held forks are
resolved-and-recorded; and the Ba-vs-follow-on scope boundary is decided. No
construction begins until the design core (the git-database approach) is settled.

## Character

Design requiring judgment, then a careful build — architectural model-setting plus the
worksite repo and its atomic manipulators. Standing now as a holding pen for settled
decisions and open forks; construction waits until the operator can focus and resolve
the git-database core. Resist building before the model sets.

content.text:
₣Ba groomed — and yes, this is unmistakably the one: the paddock's Shape line is "rebalances the fundus/curia/BURP/BURN files into the worksite model."

## Groom: ₣Ba (jjk-v4-3-create-worksite-repo)

**Heat state in one sentence:** A deliberately stabled holding pen, created 260608 with zero paces — it captures the worksite-repo design (operator-scoped, repo-shaped home shared by all projects, absorbing JJ state plus the fundus/curia/BURN/BURP files) and is waiting on you to resolve one gating fork before any paces get cut: the **worksite git-database representation** (JSON-files-in-git peer to gallops, vs. git objects/refs as the database directly).

The paddock is in good shape — eleven cinched decisions (decouple JJ state from work repos, git stays truth-and-journal, CAS-on-push as the cross-station lock, vvx as the sole git engine, soft exclusivity, BURN/BURP co-residence not absorption), and a Held list of ten forks with the git-database core explicitly named as the gate. Nothing in it reads stale against today's muster. Structurally there's nothing to do until you're ready to sit with the design core; when you are, the natural first pace is the git-database decision itself, since the paddock says it subsumes the sharding mechanism and settles the Ba-vs-follow-on scope boundary. Happy to go deeper on any fork — say the word.

## Why this was hard to discover

The honest answer is an interaction of one tool gap and two bad guesses of mine:

1. **`jjx_search` appears to be pace-anchored, and ₣Ba has zero paces.** Every scout result row is a *pace*, with the matching snippet attributed to its docket, silks, or its heat's paddock. I searched `curia`, `BURN`, and `BURP` — all three words are sitting right there in ₣Ba's paddock — and ₣Ba never appeared in any result set. With no paces to hang a hit on, the heat seems to be invisible to scout entirely. That's the load-bearing failure: the exact heats most likely to need rediscovery (paceless holding pens like this one) are the ones scout can't surface. ₣BD already has a rough pace ₢BDAAI (scout-search-rework) — this empty-heat invisibility looks like a natural addition to its scope, if you agree it's a spook worth recording.

2. **Your first keyword was the one that genuinely isn't there.** BURD appears nowhere in ₣Ba's paddock (it's the dispatch runtime, not the fleet regime), so my opening search was aimed at the wrong target — though per point 1, even the right keywords failed.

3. **The silks carry no scent.** `jjk-v4-3-create-worksite-repo` says nothing about BURN/BURP/curia — the rebalance is the heat's second clause, not its name. That's not a defect (silks name purpose, and the purpose is the worksite), but it meant the muster table gave me nothing, and I went spelunking through the *wrong* heats whose silks did pattern-match (windows, orchestration, depot-regime).

So the process you watched was me compensating for a blind search tool with paddock-by-paddock reads, steered by misleading surface cues. The fix that would have made this a ten-second answer: scout matching paddock text for paceless heats.

content.text:
## Character
Mechanical scout-engine rework — two behavior sets in one pass over `jjrsc_scout.rs` (the search loop + else-chain + match logic). Was scout-tier-and-word-frequency; absorbs the paddock-per-heat fix (formerly ₢BDAAM) because both rewrite the same search loop — separate passes would have the second re-open what the first restructured.

## Cinched — behavior set A: paddock-per-heat
`scout` surfaces a heat whose paddock matches even when the heat has zero paces. Today the paddock read is nested inside the per-pace loop (`for coronet_key in &heat.order`) and sits in the `else` of an `if silks / else if text / else if direction / else paddock` chain — so a pace-less heat is never searched, a heat whose paces all self-match never has its paddock searched, and when searched the paddock is re-read once per pace.
- Hoist the paddock search to once-per-heat, independent of pace count; read the paddock once per heat.
- Paddock-only matches render as a heat-level row (heat header + paddock excerpt, no coronet).
- The `actionable` filter applies only to pace rows — it suppresses heat-level paddock rows.
- No status-filter regression: racing, stabled, retired all stay searched.
- Spook origin: ₣BH, a 0-pace heat whose paddock saturated with its terms yet never appeared in scout.

## Cinched — behavior set B: tiering, frequency, silks-drop
- **Regex-metachar detection set:** `.*+?[](){}|^$\` — any present triggers regex mode.
- **Plain mode (no metachars):** three-tier word search — tier 1 `\bWORD\b` (whole), tier 2 `\bWORD` (prefix), tier 3 `WORD` (substring). Pace rows carry a tier provenance marker (`[whole]`/`[prefix]`/`[substring]`); results in tier order.
- **Regex mode:** single pass with the raw pattern, no permutations.
- **Word-frequency summary (always):** header block of distinct matched-text strings with occurrence counts (plain mode surfaces `ark`/`arks`/`hallmark`/… counts; regex mode counts distinct match spans).
- **Drop silks from result rows:** heat group header keeps `₣XX heat-silks`; pace rows show `₢XXXXX [state]` + excerpt only. Heat grouping retained.

## Interaction — two open questions (decide at mount)
Both sets live in the same search loop: A changes the loop's structure (paddock hoist, heat-level rows), B changes its match logic (tiering, frequency, row format). The heat-level paddock row (A) and the silks-dropped pace row (B) share one render surface — design them together. The merge leaves two behaviors undefined:
1. Does the always-on frequency summary count paddock-row match text, or only pace rows?
2. Does plain-mode 3-tier search apply to the paddock search, or only pace text?
Default lean: apply both uniformly (paddock matches feed the frequency summary; the paddock search tiers like the pace search) for consistency — confirm at mount.

## Sources
`Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjrsc_scout.rs` (loop, else-chain, match + render). `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCSC-scout.adoc` — update spec to match both behavior sets.

## Done when
- `scout ark` returns the frequency summary + tiered results with provenance markers; silks gone from pace rows.
- `scout '\bark\b'` returns single-pass regex results + frequency summary.
- A paddock-only match (incl. a 0-pace heat) yields a heat-level row; `actionable` suppresses it; no status-filter regression.
- Both open questions above are resolved in the spec. JJSCSC reflects all of the above. Build tt/vow-b, test tt/vow-t.

content.text:
Problem confirmed in both code and spec — and the repair pace **already exists**: ₢BDAAI (scout-search-rework) in ₣BD. Its "behavior set A: paddock-per-heat" is precisely this fix, absorbed from a former pace ₢BDAAM, and the spook origin recorded there is telling: *₣BH, a 0-pace heat whose paddock saturated with its terms yet never appeared in scout*. You hit this wall once before, slated the repair, and today we both rediscovered the failure live. No new pace needed — slating one would duplicate it.

What I confirmed, matching the docket's own analysis exactly:

**Code** (`Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjrsc_scout.rs:65-98`): the paddock search is nested inside `for coronet_key in &heat.order`, so a zero-pace heat never enters the loop — ₣Ba is structurally invisible. Worse, the paddock sits in the terminal `else` of the silks → text → direction chain, so a pace whose `direction` field exists but doesn't match also skips the paddock entirely, and a matching paddock is re-read and re-reported once per pace (the ~60-row ₣A6 spam we scrolled past).

**Spec** (`JJSCSC-scout.adoc`): the defect is specified, not just coded — the behavior section prescribes "For each heat → For each pace in order → search …paddock file content," so the spec itself anchors paddock search to paces. ₢BDAAI's done-when already requires JJSCSC to be rewritten to match.

**JJS0**: good news on your guess — JJS0 carries no scout behavior of its own. The operation body is a bare `include::JJSCSC-scout.adoc[]` (line 2279), and the verb definition ("Search across heats and paces") stays true under the fix. So JJSCSC is the only spec document that needs surgery; no JJS0 edit beyond what the include picks up.

Two things I found that ₢BDAAI's docket does *not* currently cover — your call whether to fold them in via redocket:

1. **Heat silks are never searched** — only pace silks (`tacks[0].silks`). A heat like ₣BF (add-tackle, placeholder paddock, 0 paces) stays unfindable even after the paddock hoist. Adding heat-silks to the heat-level match would complete the heat-level surface ₢BDAAI is already building.
2. **Field-label drift**: the code emits `spec:` and `direction:` but JJSCSC declares the field names as `docket` and `warrant`. Trivial alignment, naturally part of the spec rewrite.

Want me to redocket ₢BDAAI to add those two, or leave them for mount-time?

message.content: Do proceed with redocket as to your recommendations.

input.content:
# jjezs_reslate ₢BDAAI
## Character
Mechanical scout-engine rework — two behavior sets in one pass over `jjrsc_scout.rs` (the search loop + else-chain + match logic). Was scout-tier-and-word-frequency; absorbs the paddock-per-heat fix (formerly ₢BDAAM) because both rewrite the same search loop — separate passes would have the second re-open what the first restructured.

## Cinched — behavior set A: heat-level matching (paddock + heat silks)
`scout` surfaces a heat whose paddock or heat silks match even when the heat has zero paces. Today the paddock read is nested inside the per-pace loop (`for coronet_key in &heat.order`) and sits in the `else` of an `if silks / else if text / else if direction / else paddock` chain — so a pace-less heat is never searched, a heat whose paces all self-match never has its paddock searched, a pace whose `direction` is present-but-nonmatching skips the paddock fallback entirely, and when searched the paddock is re-read once per pace.
- Hoist the paddock search to once-per-heat, independent of pace count; read the paddock once per heat.
- Heat silks join the heat-level match surface:
  heat silks are searched nowhere today (only pace silks via `tacks[0].silks`),
  so a 0-pace heat with a placeholder paddock but matching silks stays invisible even after the paddock hoist.
- Heat-level matches (paddock or heat silks) render as a heat-level row (heat header + excerpt, no coronet).
- The `actionable` filter applies only to pace rows — it suppresses heat-level rows.
- No status-filter regression: racing, stabled, retired all stay searched.
- Spook origin: ₣BH, a 0-pace heat whose paddock saturated with its terms yet never appeared in scout. Recurred 260612: ₣Ba (paddock rich in the searched terms, zero paces) and ₣BF (matching silks, placeholder paddock, zero paces) — both undiscoverable live.

## Cinched — behavior set B: tiering, frequency, silks-drop
- **Regex-metachar detection set:** `.*+?[](){}|^$\` — any present triggers regex mode.
- **Plain mode (no metachars):** three-tier word search — tier 1 `\bWORD\b` (whole), tier 2 `\bWORD` (prefix), tier 3 `WORD` (substring). Pace rows carry a tier provenance marker (`[whole]`/`[prefix]`/`[substring]`); results in tier order.
- **Regex mode:** single pass with the raw pattern, no permutations.
- **Word-frequency summary (always):** header block of distinct matched-text strings with occurrence counts (plain mode surfaces `ark`/`arks`/`hallmark`/… counts; regex mode counts distinct match spans).
- **Drop silks from result rows:** heat group header keeps `₣XX heat-silks`; pace rows show `₢XXXXX [state]` + excerpt only. Heat grouping retained.

## Cinched — render-label alignment
Code emits `spec:` and `direction:` field labels where JJSCSC declares the field names as `docket` and `warrant` — align code to spec in the same pass (the heat-level rows mint their own labels in JJSCSC at the same time).

## Interaction — two open questions (decide at mount)
Both sets live in the same search loop: A changes the loop's structure (heat-level hoist, heat-level rows), B changes its match logic (tiering, frequency, row format). The heat-level row (A) and the silks-dropped pace row (B) share one render surface — design them together. The merge leaves two behaviors undefined:
1. Does the always-on frequency summary count heat-level match text, or only pace rows?
2. Does plain-mode 3-tier search apply to the heat-level search (paddock + heat silks), or only pace text?
Default lean: apply both uniformly (heat-level matches feed the frequency summary; the heat-level search tiers like the pace search) for consistency — confirm at mount.

## Sources
`Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/src/jjrsc_scout.rs` (loop, else-chain, match + render). `Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSCSC-scout.adoc` — update spec to match both behavior sets, the heat-level field names, and the label alignment. JJS0 needs no edit of its own: the scout operation body is a bare `include::JJSCSC-scout.adoc[]` and the verb line ("Search across heats and paces") stays true.

## Done when
- `scout ark` returns the frequency summary + tiered results with provenance markers; silks gone from pace rows.
- `scout '\bark\b'` returns single-pass regex results + frequency summary.
- A paddock-only match (incl. a 0-pace heat) yields a heat-level row; a heat-silks-only match on a 0-pace heat yields a heat-level row; `actionable` suppresses both; no status-filter regression.
- A pace with a present-but-nonmatching `direction` no longer blocks its heat's paddock from being searched.
- Field labels in result rows match JJSCSC's declared names.
- Both open questions above are resolved in the spec. JJSCSC reflects all of the above. Build tt/vow-b, test tt/vow-t.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣Ba
2	
3	## Shape
4	
5	This heat creates the **worksite repo** and the **atomic Rust manipulators** that own
6	its read/write/commit, and **rebalances the fundus/curia/BURP/BURN files** into the
7	worksite model (name provisional): an operator-scoped, repo-shaped home that any number
8	of discrete projects share. It carries an unresolved design core — chiefly the
9	worksite's git-database representation — and is stood up now to **hold** the decisions
10	and open questions below until the operator can focus on resolving them. Today's act is
11	capture, not construction; the build waits until the design core settles.
12	
13	Spine is the worksite model. BURN/BURP (fleet topology) and curia/fundus (operation
14	roles) are reorganized into it, not merely referenced.
15	
16	## Cinched
17	
18	- **Decouple JJ state from work repos** into a shared singleton worksite. The married
19	  model contaminates the work repo's git preconditions — the pensum-seed eviction is
20	  the prior symptom (JJ bookkeeping commits advanced HEAD past origin and tripped
21	  foray's curia-readiness guard). Decoupling dissolves that contamination class, not
22	  just one instance. This is the case for decoupling, weighted above merge relief.
23	- **Git stays source-of-truth and journal — not a transactional store.** The covenant
24	  survives: additive-only history, parseable commit-as-intent, jjx_log reconstruction,
25	  free immutability. A transactional store would force re-establishing all of them.
26	- **Central/shared across stations, synced by a JJ-owned pull-rebase-push loop.** The
27	  human never hand-manages the worksite's git. Git push is an atomic compare-and-swap
28	  on the remote; that CAS is the cross-station lock.
29	- **Writers must be isolatable; single-blob storage is disqualifying.** Single-blob is
30	  the shared root of merge pressure, global-lock contention, and round-trip-validation
31	  cost — and under shared-sync it fails outright (every write contends on one file;
32	  every rebase is a 3-way merge of canonical JSON). Two stations on different heats must
33	  push without conflict. Per-heat sharding is the natural mechanism; the exact
34	  representation is the git-database fork (Held) — principle is cinched, mechanism open.
35	- **Exclusivity is soft.** Reservation tokens grant node ownership; operations
36	  expire/reissue them; an operator can administratively reclaim a forgotten
37	  reservation. No distributed-consensus machinery — fits sole-operator.
38	- **Asymmetric sync.** The worksite (authoritative) is tight: gallops lifecycle ops
39	  bracket as pull-rebase -> lock -> mutate -> push -> unlock. Repos-under-test are
40	  SHA-referenced only and sync lazily (eventually-consistent).
41	- **Generalize, don't invent, for cross-repo references.** The basis field (per-tack
42	  HEAD SHA, with the unknown-sentinel for "can't determine") is already a (repo, SHA)
43	  reference — lift it to (work-repo, SHA). The legatio/BURN registry already names
44	  external targets — the work-repo registry is its sibling.
45	- **The legatio (alias, reldir) pair is the factoring:** alias -> fleet topology
46	  (leaves the repo, operator-scoped); reldir -> project location (stays with project).
47	- **BURN/BURP is BUK substrate JJ defers to**, not JJ-owned. The rebalance relocates
48	  these files into the worksite home (co-residence), but JJ resolves by alias and owns
49	  nothing of their semantics — co-residence, not absorption.
50	- **Worksite git-engine = vvx (Rust); BUK owns fleet-artifact semantics, not git
51	  mutation.** Verified grain: BUK does no git mutation today (only git status); all
52	  mutation plus the lock (refs/vvg/locks/vvx) lives in the Rust vvc crate; vvx already
53	  reads BUK's burn.env artifacts directly. One engine, one lock — two git drivers on a
54	  co-tenant store would race on a single station the way two stations would.
55	- **Read/write asymmetry — many readers, one writer.** BUK may peek the worksite, but
56	  only its own domain (fleet topology, reservation tokens) directly. Gallops awareness
57	  for BUK routes through a vvx read path — never raw JSON (Exclusive JSON Ownership).
58	  Reads take no lock and tolerate slight staleness.
59	- **Worksite + JJ core are project-agnostic, zero RBK coupling.** JJ is more general
60	  than RBK (used on any tracked project); the right relayering is extraction/delivery
61	  boundaries, NOT a runtime RBK->JJ dependency. RBK and JJ stay dependency-independent
62	  siblings on BUK. The cross-project worksite forces this. JJ's core is even
63	  BUK-independent; only foray reaches BUK artifacts.
64

file.content:
# jjezs_paddock ₣Ba

## Shape

This heat creates the **worksite repo** and the **atomic Rust manipulators** that own
its read/write/commit, and **rebalances the fundus/curia/BURP/BURN files** into the
worksite model (name provisional): an operator-scoped, repo-shaped home that any number
of discrete projects share. It carries an unresolved design core — chiefly the
worksite's git-database representation — and is stood up now to **hold** the decisions
and open questions below until the operator can focus on resolving them. Today's act is
capture, not construction; the build waits until the design core settles.

Spine is the worksite model. BURN/BURP (fleet topology) and curia/fundus (operation
roles) are reorganized into it, not merely referenced.

## Cinched

- **Decouple JJ state from work repos** into a shared singleton worksite. The married
  model contaminates the work repo's git preconditions — the pensum-seed eviction is
  the prior symptom (JJ bookkeeping commits advanced HEAD past origin and tripped
  foray's curia-readiness guard). Decoupling dissolves that contamination class, not
  just one instance. This is the case for decoupling, weighted above merge relief.
- **Git stays source-of-truth and journal — not a transactional store.** The covenant
  survives: additive-only history, parseable commit-as-intent, jjx_log reconstruction,
  free immutability. A transactional store would force re-establishing all of them.
- **Central/shared across stations, synced by a JJ-owned pull-rebase-push loop.** The
  human never hand-manages the worksite's git. Git push is an atomic compare-and-swap
  on the remote; that CAS is the cross-station lock.
- **Writers must be isolatable; single-blob storage is disqualifying.** Single-blob is
  the shared root of merge pressure, global-lock contention, and round-trip-validation
  cost — and under shared-sync it fails outright (every write contends on one file;
  every rebase is a 3-way merge of canonical JSON). Two stations on different heats must
  push without conflict. Per-heat sharding is the natural mechanism; the exact
  representation is the git-database fork (Held) — principle is cinched, mechanism open.
- **Exclusivity is soft.** Reservation tokens grant node ownership; operations
  expire/reissue them; an operator can administratively reclaim a forgotten
  reservation. No distributed-consensus machinery — fits sole-operator.
- **Asymmetric sync.** The worksite (authoritative) is tight: gallops lifecycle ops
  bracket as pull-rebase -> lock -> mutate -> push -> unlock. Repos-under-test are
  SHA-referenced only and sync lazily (eventually-consistent).
- **Generalize, don't invent, for cross-repo references.** The basis field (per-tack
  HEAD SHA, with the unknown-sentinel for "can't determine") is already a (repo, SHA)
  reference — lift it to (work-repo, SHA). The legatio/BURN registry already names
  external targets — the work-repo registry is its sibling.
- **The legatio (alias, reldir) pair is the factoring:** alias -> fleet topology
  (leaves the repo, operator-scoped); reldir -> project location (stays with project).
- **BURN/BURP is BUK substrate JJ defers to**, not JJ-owned. The rebalance relocates
  these files into the worksite home (co-residence), but JJ resolves by alias and owns
  nothing of their semantics — co-residence, not absorption.
- **Worksite git-engine = vvx (Rust); BUK owns fleet-artifact semantics, not git
  mutation.** Verified grain: BUK does no git mutation today (only git status); all
  mutation plus the lock (refs/vvg/locks/vvx) lives in the Rust vvc crate; vvx already
  reads BUK's burn.env artifacts directly. One engine, one lock — two git drivers on a
  co-tenant store would race on a single station the way two stations would.
- **Read/write asymmetry — many readers, one writer.** BUK may peek the worksite, but
  only its own domain (fleet topology, reservation tokens) directly. Gallops awareness
  for BUK routes through a vvx read path — never raw JSON (Exclusive JSON Ownership).
  Reads take no lock and tolerate slight staleness.
- **Worksite + JJ core are project-agnostic, zero RBK coupling.** JJ is more general
  than RBK (used on any tracked project); the right relayering is extraction/delivery
  boundaries, NOT a runtime RBK->JJ dependency. RBK and JJ stay dependency-independent
  siblings on BUK. The cross-project worksite forces this. JJ's core is even
  BUK-independent; only foray reaches BUK artifacts.

## Held (refine before cutting paces)

- **Worksite git-database representation — the design core that gates the build.** A
  peer to the gallops JSON (further JSON stores committed alongside), or something more
  primal (git objects/refs as the database directly). Git-stays-truth holds either way;
  this decision sets the data shape and subsumes the sharding mechanism above.
- **Scope boundary of Ba vs follow-on heats.** Which of {gallops sharding rollout,
  station-partitioned minting, the full cross-station pull-rebase-push sync loop} land
  in this heat versus later. Settled once the git-database fork resolves.
- **Advisory vs. authoritative tracked SHA.** Lean: advisory by default (degrades
  gracefully under rebase-away, as basis does today), with foray's curia-readiness
  guard the only authoritative point. Governs the entire reconciliation burden.
- **Station-partitioned firemark/coronet minting.** Root state (next_heat_seed,
  heat_order) is the irreducible shared point even with per-heat shards. Needs a
  partition scheme so heat creation requires no cross-station coordination; the
  pensum-seed "cross-officium collision acceptable" stance is the template.
- **Conflict policy** when two stations touch the same shard: CAS rejects, JJ retries a
  small number of times, then recommends an administrative action (still within the
  system). Open: what happens to a genuine content conflict on one heat-file.
- **curia/fundus re-focus.** The curia is a hub with two transports: foray/SSH to fundi
  (work execution), git to the worksite (state). Open: is the worksite a new role
  alongside curia/fundus, or curia-owned infrastructure? Reclaiming/reusing
  curia/fundus is in scope.
- **Project dimension in gallops.** With many projects sharing one worksite: does a
  heat carry an explicit project, or is project implicit via the work-repo its paces'
  SHAs reference?
- **Naming.** "worksite" and any new role term are provisional. Minting (namespace
  enumeration, terminal exclusivity, equestrian/nautical fit — note fundus already =
  Latin "estate") happens once the model sets, not now.
- **Lock-as-protocol escape hatch.** If a real "fleet op must run where vvx is absent"
  context appears, define the worksite lock as a language-agnostic protocol (lock-ref +
  CAS-on-push discipline) so a thin bash driver could participate without code-sharing
  or inverting the layer stack. Hold; do not build speculatively.
- **vvx/JJ extraction from the RBK-centric monorepo** — delivery/workspace decoupling.
  On the operator's mind; deferred.

## Done when

The worksite repo exists and its atomic Rust manipulators (validated read/write/commit
plus lock) are built and tested; the fundus/curia/BURP/BURN files are rebalanced into
the worksite model; the git-database representation and the other Held forks are
resolved-and-recorded; and the Ba-vs-follow-on scope boundary is decided. No
construction begins until the design core (the git-database approach) is settled.

## Character

Design requiring judgment, then a careful build — architectural model-setting plus the
worksite repo and its atomic manipulators. Standing now as a holding pen for settled
decisions and open forks; construction waits until the operator can focus and resolve
the git-database core. Resist building before the model sets.

content.text:
₣Ba groomed fresh — unchanged since this morning: a stabled, zero-pace holding pen whose design core (the worksite's git-database representation) gates everything; nothing gets built until that fork resolves.

The Held list is your discussion menu, one line each:

1. **Git-database representation** — JSON shards committed alongside, vs. git objects/refs as the database directly. The gate; subsumes sharding.
2. **Ba scope boundary** — which of {sharding rollout, partitioned minting, full sync loop} land here vs. follow-on heats.
3. **Advisory vs. authoritative tracked SHA** — lean is advisory-by-default.
4. **Station-partitioned minting** — root state (heat seed/order) is the irreducible shared point.
5. **Conflict policy** — CAS-reject/retry is set; genuine content conflict on one heat-file is open.
6. **Curia/fundus re-focus** — is the worksite a third role, or curia-owned infrastructure?
7. **Project dimension** — explicit on the heat, or implicit via work-repo SHAs?
8. **Naming** — all provisional until the model sets.
9. **Lock-as-protocol escape hatch** — held, don't build.
10. **vvx/JJ extraction from the monorepo** — deferred, on your mind.

The paddock itself says #1 is where focus should go first, and #2 falls out of it. Where do you want to start?

content: We're grooming heat ₣Ba, the worksite-repo design holding pen, to discuss its open design forks. Next action: pick a fork to discuss, starting with the gating one, the worksite's git-database representation.

message.content: I'm not ready to take on that list right now.  I did have a stray thought to land: I wonder if this new 'worksite' idea has the shape of a whole new kit, as in JJK/RBK/BUK?  I don't have clarity of what is inside it, but I do muse the 'worksite' git repo is in a gitignored subdirectory.  There might be tabtargets related to this homed here.  Hmm, another tangent: the name 'worksite' isn't excellent given its overlap with 'workbench' which is a key notion in BUK.  Oh, the new 'kit' I'm envisioning would indeed depend upoon BUK, but not JJK;  JJK would depend on the new kit.  Thoughts?

content.text:
Good musings — and I think the kit shape isn't just plausible, it's nearly forced by what's already cinched. Let me walk it:

**The inversion forces a new home.** You want JJK to depend on the worksite, so the worksite can't live inside JJK. BUK can't own it either — your cinch says BUK does no git mutation, and the worksite is *made of* git mutation. So by elimination it needs a home that sits above BUK and below JJK in the dependency stack. That's exactly a kit-shaped hole. The one existing alternative is VVK — it already owns the vvc git machinery and the lock ref, and "core infrastructure for Claude Code kits" isn't a crazy charter for it — but my lean is VVK stays the generic *engine* kit and the new kit owns worksite *semantics* (fleet topology, reservations, the sync loop, the work-repo registry), delivered through the vvx binary the way jjx already is. Kit identity and binary identity are already decoupled in this codebase; that precedent does the work.

**The layering survives your JJ-core-independence cinch, with one refinement.** "JJ's core is even BUK-independent" looks threatened by JJK → newkit → BUK, but the new kit would have the same internal split JJK already has: a Rust core (manipulators, lock, sync — no BUK) and a bash/tabtarget surface (workbench, launchers — BUK). JJ core consumes the Rust API; the BUK dependency rides only the operator-facing surface. Worth stating explicitly when this lands in the paddock, because it sharpens rather than weakens the existing cinch.

**Gitignored subdirectory + tabtargets homed here — two readings worth holding apart.** The kit *source* (Tools/«kit»/, tt/ tabtargets) is ordinary and uncontroversial. The worksite *clone* location is the interesting part: a gitignored subdir of each work repo means one clone per work repo, which actually fits your asymmetric-sync cinch nicely — clones are disposable caches, the remote CAS is the only authority, and relative paths keep tooling simple. The alternative is one operator-scoped clone (~-anchored). I'd hold both; the choice interacts with the "project dimension" Held item.

**On the name: agree, and I'd record the constraint now even though minting waits.** "Worksite" shares the `work-` stem with workbench, one of BUK's most load-bearing ashlars, and "work" is trodden vocabulary anyway. The durable note is negative: *whatever the mint, avoid the work- stem*. (If you want a flavor of where the register could go: equestrian has *livery* — a livery yard boards many owners' horses under one roof, which is precisely an operator-scoped home shared by many projects. But that's a mull for the naming pace, not a decision.)

One counterweight, for honesty: a kit costs real overhead — prefix allocation, zipper, workbench, context file, delivery story — and the litmus is whether it has more than one consumer. I think it passes (JJK consumes it, BUK co-resides and peeks, future fleet ops land there), but if it ever shrinks to "JJK's storage layer," the kit ceremony was overweight and a vvx-internal module would have done.

Want me to land this in the ₣Ba paddock — a new Held entry for kit-ification (with the VVK alternative noted), the clone-location fork folded into it, and the work- stem anti-constraint added to the Naming item?

message.content: The kit would keep the shape of a weird resposibility I want strongly controlled: both of the prior systems 'like' having a clean way to create precise users on remote systems and manage their keying environment for code access and normalization for extremely reproducable testing and simultaneous operation: it would be so good for the 100m tests to be done in parallel where possible between windows and macos and linux!  But this is all code that I want written at systems code caliber, BCG or better.  Thoughts?

content.content:
== Word Selection

Vocabulary is the primary interface of every system the
{mcm_editor}
choreographs:
names are how intent survives across minds and sessions,
and the specification machinery around them is scaffolding in their service.
A name that fails its reader fails the system,
however sound the code beneath it.

Prefix discipline governs the left half of every persistent name;
this section governs the right half — the word itself.
The rules below bind the word part of every
{mcm_quoin},
the display text of every
{mcm_linked_term},
and any persistent name a system mints: verbs, nouns, file words, command words.
They constrain births, not the living —
existing names are swept only by deliberate remint tooling, never piecemeal.

* *Semantic uniqueness.*
One meaning per word across all persistent names, repo-wide.
A word already bound to a meaning anywhere in the system is unavailable for a second meaning, however distant the domain.
* *One word per concept.*
The dual of semantic uniqueness: no alias
{mcm_quoin_s} —
a concept already named is closed to a second word.
Near neighbors must be spaced:
adjacent definitions state their distinction, or one of the pair dies.
* *Vocabulary isolation.*
Words mint from source domains alien to software, cloud infrastructure, CI/CD, and corporate project management.
A word with heavy ambient presence in software prose is a
{mcm_trodden_word}
and is disqualified regardless of semantic fit.
Domain choice makes collision structurally impossible;
word-level checks merely verify.
* *The grep gate.*
A candidate word is verified by repo-wide grep before adoption;
a clean landing is a minting precondition, not a nicety.
* *Rare but real.*
Prefer rare, real words whose true meaning does semantic work.
Concrete, physical, sensory images lodge across sessions in ways abstract labels cannot.
Abbreviations spend recognition to save characters — the wrong trade.
* *Word husbandry.*
A word's commonness matches its operation's traffic:
daily surfaces earn short, plain, instantly-guessable words;
rare ceremonies must not spend them —
mint longer, rarer, more colorful words there,
conserving the small stock of plain English for the surfaces that need it.
Husbandry lowers the commonness ceiling, never the
{mcm_fair_faced}
floor: color may rise with rarity; opacity may not.
* *Sibling initials.*
Wherever a sibling set shares an abbreviation namespace —
colophon suffixes, quoin sub-letters, any single-character discriminator —
elect words whose first letters stay distinct,
so the abbreviation can remain the word's initial.
The verbs orbiting one critical noun are siblings from birth,
abbreviation namespace or not:
verb families acquire one eventually, and by then the words are minted.
An election-time check beside the grep gate;
the word-side twin of sub-letter monosemy.
* *Mint into an* {mcm_asterism}.
A new word joins a coherent metaphor family wherever one fits;
a lone orphan word is weaker than a family member.
* *Audible register.*
Sibling subsystems ride
{mcm_asterism_s}
whose registers are distinguishable by ear,
so a stray term's home system is audible without lookup.

[[mcm_asterism]]
{mcm_asterism}::
A named metaphor family whose source-domain relationships mirror the system relationships it names.
The name is the gestalt: a recognizable figure of stars that is not itself a constellation and may span several —
as a vocabulary family may span
{mcm_concept_model_s}.
Each term inherits relational meaning from the family
(the coronet sits below the firemark as the hoof marking sits below the brand),
so the metaphor performs structural work beyond retrieval,
and a new term in an established family arrives nearly pre-learned —
for the
{mcm_digital_mind}
and the
{mcm_editor}
alike.
Founding specimen: Job Jockey's equestrian vocabulary (JJS0, Equestrian Vocabulary), with the racing and dressage layers as sibling families sharing one register.
Model-side rationale: `Memos/retired/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`;
prefix-side sibling: `Memos/memo-20260110-acronym-selection-study.md`.

=== Exposure

Word-selection care scales with who must read the name.
Every persistent name sits on a ladder of increasing dressing:

* {mcm_hearting} — interior names, uncatalogued, governed by prefix discipline alone.
* A
{mcm_quoin}
— the catalogued default; the rules above bind.
* {mcm_ashlar} — a
{mcm_quoin}
that faces the operator; everything below binds, plus the rules at its definition.

The ladder is deliberate at both ends:
{mcm_hearting}
is permission to leave interior names plain,
granted so that care can concentrate where reading is costly.
Misclassification fails quiet in one direction —
a word that should be
{mcm_ashlar}
but is unmarked skips the stricter tests —
so when a word surfaces in operator output, ask whether it belongs on the
{mcm_broadside}.

[[mcm_ashlar]]
{mcm_ashlar}::
A
{mcm_quoin}
the operator must trigger, observe in output or errors, or reason about system state with.
The dressed facing stone — finished finely because it faces out.
Beyond the rules above, an
{mcm_ashlar}
must be
{mcm_fair_faced},
draws from the
{mcm_coffer},
and is not minted until it appears on the project's
{mcm_broadside}.
Scrutiny scales with contact frequency:
a word the operator meets daily outranks a founding-ceremony word,
and both outrank any spec-interior
{mcm_quoin}.
Verbs name what the operator does or sees, never the mechanism beneath.
Words appearing in failure output are
{mcm_ashlar_s}
even when the operator never invokes them.

[[mcm_hearting]]
{mcm_hearting}::
The wall-core fill — structural, unseen, taking no dressing.
Interior names governed by prefix discipline alone:
locals, private helpers, intermediate artifacts no operator and no spec must read.
{mcm_hearting}
is a deliberate grant of plainness, not neglect;
its existence is what lets
{mcm_ashlar}
care stay affordable.

[[mcm_fair_faced]]
{mcm_fair_faced}::
The property an
{mcm_ashlar}
must hold: finished so it needs no render coat.
An operator meeting the word for the first time, mid-ceremony,
must act correctly without a glossary lookup.
Rare enough to land clean in the grep gate,
common enough to guess warm —
the deliberate tightening of rare-but-real at the wall's face.
The
{mcm_cold_probe}
is its test.

[[mcm_coffer]]
{mcm_coffer}::
The bounded budget of
{mcm_ashlar_s}
an operator can be asked to hold.
Each mint spends from it;
spec-grain precision collapses into fewer operator words wherever the distinction does not change what the operator does.
The fine grain lives on in
{mcm_quoin_s};
the
{mcm_coffer}
governs only the wall's face.

[[mcm_broadside]]
{mcm_broadside}::
The single public sheet where a project posts its
{mcm_ashlar}
vocabulary.
Each project designates exactly one;
presence on the
{mcm_broadside}
is the registry contract —
an
{mcm_ashlar}
mint is incomplete until its entry exists.
A project whose operators work through digital minds may mirror the sheet into agent context files;
mirrors are derived, never canonical.

=== Exposure

Word-selection care scales with who must read the name.
Every persistent name sits on a ladder of increasing dressing:

* {mcm_hearting} — interior names, uncatalogued, governed by prefix discipline alone.
* A
{mcm_quoin}
— the catalogued default; the rules above bind.
* {mcm_ashlar} — a
{mcm_quoin}
that faces the operator; everything below binds, plus the rules at its definition.

The ladder is deliberate at both ends:
{mcm_hearting}
is permission to leave interior names plain,
granted so that care can concentrate where reading is costly.
Misclassification fails quiet in one direction —
a word that should be
{mcm_ashlar}
but is unmarked skips the stricter tests —
so when a word surfaces in operator output, ask whether it belongs on the
{mcm_broadside}.

[[mcm_ashlar]]
{mcm_ashlar}::
A
{mcm_quoin}
the operator must trigger, observe in output or errors, or reason about system state with.
The dressed facing stone — finished finely because it faces out.
Beyond the rules above, an
{mcm_ashlar}
must be
{mcm_fair_faced},
draws from the
{mcm_coffer},
and is not minted until it appears on the project's
{mcm_broadside}.
Scrutiny scales with contact frequency:
a word the operator meets daily outranks a founding-ceremony word,
and both outrank any spec-interior
{mcm_quoin}.
Verbs name what the operator does or sees, never the mechanism beneath.
Words appearing in failure output are
{mcm_ashlar_s}
even when the operator never invokes them.

[[mcm_hearting]]
{mcm_hearting}::
The wall-core fill — structural, unseen, taking no dressing.
Interior names governed by prefix discipline alone:
locals, private helpers, intermediate artifacts no operator and no spec must read.
{mcm_hearting}
is a deliberate grant of plainness, not neglect;
its existence is what lets
{mcm_ashlar}
care stay affordable.

[[mcm_fair_faced]]
{mcm_fair_faced}::
The property an
{mcm_ashlar}
must hold: finished so it needs no render coat.
An operator meeting the word for the first time, mid-ceremony,
must act correctly without a glossary lookup.
Rare enough to land clean in the grep gate,
common enough to guess warm —
the deliberate tightening of rare-but-real at the wall's face.
The
{mcm_cold_probe}
is its test.

[[mcm_coffer]]
{mcm_coffer}::
The bounded budget of
{mcm_ashlar_s}
an operator can be asked to hold.
Each mint spends from it;
spec-grain precision collapses into fewer operator words wherever the distinction does not change what the operator does.
The fine grain lives on in
{mcm_quoin_s};
the
{mcm_coffer}
governs only the wall's face.

[[mcm_broadside]]
{mcm_broadside}::
The single public sheet where a project posts its
{mcm_ashlar}
vocabulary.
Each project designates exactly one;
presence on the
{mcm_broadside}
is the registry contract —
an
{mcm_ashlar}
mint is incomplete until its entry exists.
A project whose operators work through digital minds may mirror the sheet into agent context files;
mirrors are derived, never canonical.

content.text:
Thoughts first, then the soak.

## On the charter

**The containment argument is the strongest one yet for kit-hood.** Code that creates users on remote machines and installs key material is the most dangerous code in your estate — more dangerous than anything in RBK's cloud path, because it holds privileged SSH to every node you own. One kit means one audit perimeter, one declared caliber bar, one place where "strongly controlled" is enforceable rather than aspirational. That beats the dependency-layering argument I gave earlier; layering says the kit is *convenient*, containment says it's *load-bearing*.

**But notice what this charter does to a standing cinch.** ₣Ba currently says "BURN/BURP is BUK substrate JJ defers to — co-residence, not absorption." Your description — the kit *owns* user creation, keying, normalization — is garrison. That's absorption: ₣A-'s jurisdiction verbs (caparison/invigilate/garrison) and their regimes migrate out of BUK into the new kit, and BUK returns to being a pure utility kit with no fleet semantics at all. I think that's the better architecture and your instinct is ahead of the paddock, but it's a cinch revision, not an addition — worth saying out loud before we write it in.

**On caliber:** BCG-or-better decomposes naturally by surface. The state core (git database, lock, CAS sync, manipulators) is already cinched Rust — that's RCG. The remote-transport surfaces stay bash at BCG+WSG, and I'd resist any urge to rewrite them in Rust for purity: the Windows transport stack is a Palisade membrane built from hard-won empirical knowledge (the chit capture, UTF-16LE drops, forced-command quirks — a whole memo lineage). The caliber bar there is WSG conformance, not language change.

**The parallel-test vision quietly completes the multi-consumer case.** Reservation tokens + normalized fundi are exactly a fleet scheduler's substrate, which makes theurge/RBK suites a consumer alongside JJK — the kit stops being "JJK's storage layer" for good. And note the existing "sequential only" rule is *per-station* (shared regime state and container namespaces); cross-node parallelism dodges it by construction. Windows ∥ macOS ∥ Linux each running their serial ladder simultaneously is consistent with every current constraint.

## The naming soak

MCM's rules that bite here: source domain alien to software; rare-but-real, concrete; mint into an asterism whose *relationships mirror the system's*; register audible against the siblings (JJK equestrian, RBK occult/foundry — and warning, RBK's federation heat is actively colonizing Anglo-Norman manorial: manor, terrier, enfranchise, escheat — so demesne/grange/bailiwick are off the table on the audible-register rule, clean greps notwithstanding).

Two directions worth soaking:

**1. Roman estate administration — extend the family that already exists.** JJK's foray vocabulary is already Latin-administrative: *curia*, *legatio*, *pensum*, *fundus*. And fundus means *estate*. The responsibility you described — an absent owner's estates run by a trusted overseer who provisions exact laborers, entrusts them keys, and keeps every holding worked to one standard — is literally the Roman *villicatio*. The family: **vilicus** (the overseer himself — the kit's role-name), **latifundium** (one administration over many fundi — the worksite/estate-system), **peculium** (property entrusted to a dependent — the provisioned user's keyed environment; this one does startling semantic work). All grep-clean. The MCM payoff is that fundus arrives *already a member* — "a new term in an established family arrives nearly pre-learned" — and the vocabulary migrates to the kit exactly as the responsibility does.

**2. Falconry — found a fresh asterism.** The **mews**: the building where trained hawks are kept between flights, each hooded, jessed, weathered to an exact standard, flown in parallel and returned to the fist. Mews = the worksite home; **jess** = the key tether (grep-clean — the 4 hits are name-dictionary data); **creance** = the training line for an unproven environment. Short, concrete, fair-faced words; register unmistakable against every sibling kit. Weaker structural mirror than the Roman family (nodes-as-hawks takes a squint; the Roman one needs none), but the freshest by ear.

My lean is the Roman family — MCM rewards joining an established asterism, and the fundus inheritance is structural, not decorative. But soaking means you live with them a while; try saying "charge the latifundium," "garrison the peculium onto rocket," "the vilicus reclaims the reservation" and see which survives your morning voice. Pronunciation friction on *latifundium* is real and might matter for a daily word — husbandry says the daily surfaces deserve the short words (fundus, peculium are fine; latifundium may be a founding-ceremony word only).

When you're ready, I'll fold the charter expansion (containment, jurisdiction absorption as a cinch revision, caliber-by-surface, parallel-test consumer) into the ₣Ba paddock, with the name left explicitly soaking.

content: We're shaping ₣Ba (worksite repo) into a new kit owning remote-user provisioning and keying, with candidate names soaking (Roman estate family vs falconry). Next action: fold the expanded charter into the ₣Ba paddock when you give the word.

message.content:
re: "But notice what this charter does to a standing cinch."  Noted!  And yes I want our update to commit to this path.

Weirdly I'm more drawn to the Falconry anchor gestalt...  It creates some space from the RB 'civics' world and more closely ties this new kit to job jockey thematically, and I sort of like that.  Yes, this uproots fundus and curia and lagatio I know... I'm not sure how I feel about that but I'm prepared to reword the paddock for a few revisions to that to try it on.

Thoughts before we make a paddock amendment?  I'm getting tired so I can't remember if we discussed anything that deserves capture in a memo here, advice?

content.text:
Three thoughts, then memo advice — keeping this short since you're fading.

**1. Falconry doesn't have to uproot anything yet.** MCM is explicit: word selection "constrains births, not the living — existing names are swept only by deliberate remint tooling, never piecemeal." Fundus/curia/legatio are living JJK words. The right paddock posture is: falconry is the new kit's *elected register for new mints*; the foray-word remint is a held item that fires when the responsibility actually migrates. You get to try the register on for several revisions without committing to the sweep — which is exactly the soak you want.

**2. There's a historical bridge that makes this choice better than it first looks.** The King's Mews — the actual building — was a hawk mews that *became the royal stables*. The word itself migrated from falconry to the equestrian world. A kit founded on the mews, sitting under Job Jockey's stables, is riding a real etymological relationship, not a forced one. That's the asterism rule working at its best: the source-domain relationship mirrors the system relationship.

**3. Hold the legend loose in the amendment.** Fix only the anchor points that earned it today — *mews* = the operator-scoped home, *jess* = the key tether binding a provisioned user — and leave the rest (what exactly is the hawk? what's a cast?) explicitly unminted. Premature legend-filling is the same failure as building before the design core settles.

**Memo advice: yes, one memo, and it's the naming deliberation — not the architecture.** The architecture (absorption cinch revision, containment charter, caliber-by-surface, parallel-fleet consumer) all belongs in the paddock; that's shape. But today's register survey — manorial rejected for RBK-federation register collision, nautical dead on the vessel collision, Roman surveyed with its fundus-inheritance argument, falconry elected with grep gates run — is deliberation rationale that the paddock shouldn't carry and you won't reconstruct in a month. That's exactly the pattern of the existing minting memos (MCM itself cites them for asterism rationale). One short `memo-20260612-«kit»-falconry-asterism-soak.md`, paddock points at it.

Say go and I'll write both: the memo, then the paddock amendment committing to the absorption path with falconry soaking and the foray-remint held.

input.content:
# Falconry asterism soak — naming deliberation for the ₣Ba kit

**Heat:** ₣Ba
**Date:** 2026-06-12
**Status:** SOAKING — register elected, anchors fixed, legend deliberately unminted.
**Inputs:** MCM § Word Selection; CLAUDE.md Prefix Naming Discipline;
`Memos/memo-20260110-acronym-selection-study.md`; ₣Ba paddock (same-day revision).

---

## What needed naming

₣Ba founds a new kit between BUK and JJK: an operator-scoped, repo-shaped shared
state home ("worksite", placeholder) plus the strongly-controlled fleet-provisioning
responsibility (precise remote users, keying, normalization for reproducible
parallel testing) absorbed from BUK jurisdiction. The kit needs a register
(MCM asterism) before any of its persistent names can mint. The placeholder
"worksite" was disqualified on inspection: the `work-` stem collides audibly with
*workbench*, one of BUK's most load-bearing ashlars, and "work" is trodden
vocabulary besides. Standing anti-constraint regardless of final mint: **avoid the
`work-` stem entirely.**

## MCM rules that did the work

- *Vocabulary isolation* — source domain alien to software/cloud/CI/corporate PM.
- *Audible register* — sibling kits must be distinguishable by ear
  (JJK equestrian; RBK occult/foundry, with the federation heat actively
  colonizing Anglo-Norman manorial/legal: manor, terrier, enfranchise, escheat).
- *Mint into an asterism* — source-domain relationships must mirror system
  relationships, not merely decorate.
- *Constrains births, not the living* — an elected register governs NEW mints;
  existing words sweep only via deliberate remint, never piecemeal.
- *The grep gate* — run before adoption (results below).

## Register survey

| Register | Specimens | Disposition |
|---|---|---|
| English manorial | demesne, grange, bailiwick, croft, steading, livery | **Rejected.** Greps clean, but RBK's federation work is colonizing the adjacent Anglo-Norman manorial/legal register — fails audible-register against a sibling. `livery` additionally drowns in `delivery` substring noise (34 hits). |
| Nautical / dockyard | berth, roadstead, chandlery, bosun | **Rejected.** "Vessel" is load-bearing RBK vocabulary (container images); a ship register collides head-on. (`rbmm_moorings` shows the shoreline is already partially occupied.) |
| Roman estate administration | vilicus, latifundium, peculium, villicatio | **Runner-up, seriously surveyed.** Strong structural mirror (absent owner's estates run by a trusted overseer who provisions exact laborers and entrusts keys — *peculium* is the keyed environment, startlingly apt). Inherits JJK's existing Latin foray family (curia, legatio, pensum, fundus = "estate") pre-learned. Rejected by operator preference: too close to RB's civics/Latin world; the new kit should pull *toward* Job Jockey thematically, not toward RB. Pronunciation friction on the daily-traffic candidates noted. |
| Falconry | mews, jess, creance, hood, weathering, cast | **ELECTED for soak.** Country-pursuit register adjacent to JJK's equestrian without blurring into it; maximal distance from RB's registers. Etymological bridge does real asterism work: the King's Mews was a hawk mews that *became the royal stables* — the word itself migrated from falconry to the equestrian world, mirroring a kit that sits beneath Job Jockey. Concrete, sensory, fair-faced-capable short words. |

## Grep gates (run 2026-06-12, Tools/ + Memos/ + README)

| Word | Hits | Reading |
|---|---|---|
| mews | 0 | clean |
| creance | 0 | clean |
| jess | 4 | all name-dictionary data (jesse/jessica in APCK wordlists) — no semantic binding; effectively clean |
| cast | 191 | trodden (Rust casts) — **disqualified** as a mint despite falconry fit ("a cast of hawks" = two flown together) |
| vilicus / latifundium / peculium / villicatio | 0 | clean (recorded for the runner-up) |
| demesne / grange / bailiwick / steading / croft | 0 | clean but register-rejected |
| livery | 34 | `delivery` substring noise — disqualified |

## Fixed anchors (the only points minted into the soak)

- **mews** — the operator-scoped, repo-shaped home where provisioned environments
  are kept between flights. Replaces the "worksite" placeholder in paddock prose.
- **jess** — the key tether binding a provisioned user (the strap by which a
  trained hawk is held and released).

Everything else is deliberately unminted. In particular: what exactly is the hawk
(the provisioned user-environment on a node is the leading reading), what a flight
is, the kit's own name and 2–4 char prefix (namespace enumeration + terminal
exclusivity per CLAUDE.md minting workflow), and every verb.

## Open questions the soak must answer

1. Does the register survive daily voice? ("charge the mews", "jess the workload
   user onto rocket") — operator to live with it across several paddock revisions.
2. Disposition of the living foray words (fundus, curia, legatio, pensum): they
   stay JJK-owned and untouched during the soak (births-not-living). If the kit
   absorbs the responsibility they name, a deliberate remint pace decides whether
   they migrate, translate into falconry, or stand as a JJK-side dialect.
3. The hawk mapping — settle before the first verb mints, since verbs orbit it
   (sibling-initials rule applies to the verb family from birth).
4. Ashlar budget: which falconry words face the operator (broadside registration
   required) vs stay quoin/hearting.

## Lineage

Manorial-rejection evidence: ₣BZ paddock (office-federation conversion, same day).
Roman-family argument preserved above in case the soak fails and the runner-up
returns. Asterism doctrine: MCM § Word Selection; model-side rationale precedent:
`Memos/retired/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`.
